checks.a_analysis: The output was compared against the six-page source document. All paragraphs, headings, and list items in the JSON correspond to content found in the original document. No invented content or fabricated sections were identified.
The source document contains one URL. In the output, this URL is missing entirely rather than being present but incorrect. No fabricated URLs, phone numbers, or emails were found.

All nine numbered sections from the source document are represented in the JSON output, and all six pages of the document have been processed. While specific text elements are missing (see C5), no entire pages or sections were omitted.
The source document contains no tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are three major omissions of content. First, the subsection headings 'a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and 'b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data' from item 3 on page 1 are missing. Second, a technical URL in the first paragraph of page 3 is missing. Third, and most significant, the output omits the three detailed sub-bullet items under 'Source data questions:' in section 6 on page 5, which contain critical technical questions for consideration.
checks.b_evidence_source:
1) 'a. 911/NG911 in the Media' and 'b. AddressNC and Census Geospatial Data' (Page 1)
2) '(http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf)' (Page 3)
3) 'o Is current satellite imagery... o Would color infrared imagery... o Would National Agriculture Imagery Program...' (Page 5)
checks.b_evidence_output:
1) Output skips from 'Quick Updates' to body text.
2) Paragraph ends at '...locations for GIS'
3) Item 5 in list element 43 contains only the text 'Source data questions:' with no nested items.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Excluding the significant omissions noted in C5, the text content accurately mirrors the source document with standard reformatting for structured data.

checks.a_analysis: The output document successfully processes the multi-page source without repeating sections or paragraphs. Each block of text from the source appears exactly once in the structured JSON, following the page flow.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent and contains skips. The main numbered items (1-9) which are stylistically identical in the source are assigned varying levels: items 1-3 are in an unordered list, item 4 is H2, items 5-6 are H3 (skipping H2 in that section), item 7 is H2, and items 8-9 are H3 (skipping H2). Furthermore, subsection headings like 'b. Orthoimagery' are assigned H3, while similar subsections on page 1 (3a, 3b) are treated as regular paragraphs.
checks.b_evidence_source: 5. GICC Website (Page 4); 6. Committee Reports (Page 4); 8. Other Items (Page 6); 9. Future Meeting Dates (Page 6)
checks.b_evidence_output: {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "5. GICC Website"}; {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "6. Committee Reports"}; {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "8. Other Items"}; {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "9. Future Meeting Dates"}

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains only narrative text, headings, and lists. There are no tables to evaluate.
The reading order in the JSON correctly follows the logical flow of the meeting minutes from page 1 through page 6, including text that spans across page breaks.

There are multiple list issues. First, the numbered items 1, 2, and 3 on page 1 are converted into an unordered list, losing their numbering. More significantly, the nested bulleted list on page 5 under 'Source data questions:' is partially missing; the five primary bullet points are present, but the three sub-bullets (o) containing specific source data questions are completely absent from the structured output.
checks.b_evidence_source: o Is current satellite imagery... o Would color infrared imagery... o Would National Agriculture Imagery Program... (Page 5)
checks.b_evidence_output: The list in reading_order 43 ends after the item 'Source data questions:' with no nested items provided.

checks.a_analysis: A URL for a PDF document is clearly visible in the source on page 3 but is rendered only as plain text in the output JSON without being wrapped in a link object or having associated link metadata.
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "A survey in 2010 found a variety of departmental locations for GIS (http://www.ncgicc.org/Portals/3/documents/Location_of_County_GIS_Operations.pdf)."

checks.a_analysis: The HTML rendering faithfully represents the layout of the source PDF. The source is a multi-page text-based minutes document with numbered and lettered sections. The HTML converts this into a single-column flow with an added sidebar table of contents for navigation, which is an expected accessibility feature. The hierarchical structure (1., 2., 3.a., 3.b., etc.) is preserved correctly. Information is located in the same relative positions as the original.

Upon review of the source PDF, there are no tables present in this document. The content consists entirely of headings, paragraphs, and nested lists. Therefore, there are no tables to be misrendered in the HTML.
The source PDF is a text-only document. There are no meaningful images, charts, diagrams, or logos present in the original. The HTML output correctly reflects this absence of imagery.

A thorough visual scan of the HTML output confirms that no body content (paragraphs, list items) is duplicated. While heading text appears in both the Table of Contents and the document body, this is an intentional and expected component of the accessible design and not a defect.
